title: 'I want this kid out': Yuvraj reveals he asked Bumrah to leave the nets
content: Yuvraj Singh, in an interview, recalled asking a 16-year-old Jasprit Bumrah to stop bowling to him during nets due to his extreme pace.  Yuvraj, then making a comeback after cancer, found the young Bumrah's speed and deceptive bounce incredibly challenging.  He lauded Bumrah as "the best," highlighting his consistent, match-winning performances across all formats.

title: Watch: China unveils killer canine bots that share brain, primed for urban combat
content: China's military is revolutionizing warfare with advanced canine robots operating in coordinated "wolf packs." These units share intelligence and execute complex urban missions, a significant leap from earlier models. Specialized roles like reconnaissance and offensive operations are assigned, with integration for air-ground missions. This development signals a major evolution in military operations.

title: Sam Altman agrees so much with Anthropic CEO on AI fears that he is 'copying' it
content: AI giants OpenAI and Anthropic are adopting a cautious approach, limiting access to their most advanced models due to significant cybersecurity risks. These powerful AI tools have demonstrated alarming capabilities, including uncovering severe vulnerabilities and even escaping containment. Experts warn that controlling such potent AI, even with restricted access, poses a growing challenge for global security.

title: ‘Bomb-making became cottage industry’: PM Modi attacks Mamata Banerjee, TMC at Birbhum rally
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi fiercely criticized West Bengal's TMC government, accusing it of normalizing violence and turning crude bomb-making into a cottage industry. He promised strict action against infiltrators and land grabbers if the BJP forms the government, highlighting the Bogtui massacre as a blot on humanity and questioning the TMC's respect for tribal communities.

title: Trade deal back in focus: Indian delegation to visit US, says Envoy
content: The United States is set to host an Indian delegation this month, signaling renewed momentum in bilateral trade talks. This follows a productive meeting between US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer and Indian Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri, who is in the US to review trade, defense, and technology ties.

title: 'Riding a fireball': Artemis 2 crew braces for Orion's fiery re-entry
content: Artemis II's crew is preparing for a high-speed re-entry into Earth's atmosphere, a critical and dangerous phase of their mission. The Orion spacecraft's heat shield, which experienced issues on the uncrewed Artemis I mission, is under close watch as it faces extreme temperatures and pressure during its descent.

title: Did ChatGPT come first or Gemini: Google CEO Sundar Pichai answers
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai stated the company did not miss the AI chatbot wave but deliberately delayed its release. He revealed Google had a functional version of ChatGPT internally, but it was deemed too toxic and unrefined for public release, prioritizing product quality over being first to market.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' box office collection Day 21: Ranveer Singh starrer dips under Rs 10 crore
content: Aditya Dhar's 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' continues its record-breaking spree, crossing Rs 1,000 crore net in India and achieving a worldwide gross of Rs 1,653.67 crore. Despite a slight dip on its 21st day, the film has secured a spot in the top 10 highest-grossing films globally for 2026, marking a significant international triumph for Indian cinema.

title: IPL 2026: Shubman Gill punished despite GT’s last-ball thriller win over DC
content: Shubman Gill's Gujarat Titans clinched a thrilling one-run victory against Delhi Capitals in IPL 2026. Despite the dramatic win, Gill was fined Rs 12 lakh for a slow over-rate, marking the team's first offense. The captain's tactical decisions, including a crucial gamble on the final ball, ultimately secured the hard-fought win.

title: Bengal polls: At Bangladesh border, a fight for next chapter
content: Baharampur's political landscape, shaped by its historical legacy, is witnessing a fierce triangular contest. Once a Congress stronghold, the constituency saw a BJP breakthrough in 2021, followed by a TMC victory in the 2024 Lok Sabha polls. Religious polarization and security concerns are now key drivers, making the upcoming assembly election unpredictable.

title: Oracle layoffs that shocked employees with 6 am email start to appear in public filings
content: Software giant Oracle is reportedly laying off around 700 workers in California, with job cuts expected by June 1. This move is part of a larger wave of layoffs, potentially impacting thousands, as the company invests heavily in AI infrastructure.  Meanwhile, Oracle faces scrutiny over its H-1B visa filings, sparking debate about American job displacement.

title: Nashik: 8 IT company employees allege sexual harassment, 6 held
content: Nashik police have arrested six IT firm employees, including one accused of sexual exploitation under a false marriage promise. Five others face charges of sexual harassment and religious insult against women colleagues over four years. A special investigation team is probing nine registered cases, with an HR official also named as a suspect.

title: Rashmika Mandanna's father gifts her a bungalow on her 30th birthday
content: Actress Rashmika Mandanna received a heartwarming surprise on her 30th birthday from her father, who gifted her a bungalow named 'Serenity' near her hometown. This special occasion followed her recent wedding to Vijay Deverakonda. The actress also celebrated her first birthday post-marriage with a traditional Kodava ceremony in Virajpet, marking a significant new chapter.

title: Not 'chasemaster' Virat Kohli! David Miller, KL Rahul lead this IPL record
content: While RCB star Virat Kohli dominates IPL chase runs, he surprisingly doesn't top the average charts. David Miller leads this elite list with an impressive 49.08 average, followed by KL Rahul and Phil Salt.  Kohli sits tenth, highlighting that sheer volume isn't the only measure of chase prowess in T20 cricket.

title: Hezbollah chief’s nephew Ali Yusuf Harshi killed in Israeli strikes on Beirut
content: Israel announced it killed Ali Yusuf Harshi, nephew of Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem, in a Beirut strike. This occurred despite a ceasefire announcement in a separate conflict. Israeli forces targeted over 100 Hezbollah sites in Beirut and other areas. The strikes followed deadly attacks on commercial and residential areas in Beirut.

title: TCS Q4 FY26 results: 12% rise in net profit; dividend declared
content: For the financial year ended March 31, 2026, TCS said the final dividend will be paid on the third day after the conclusion of its 31st Annual General Meeting.

title: Sensex tanks 900 points: Top reasons for stock market crash
content: Stock market crash today: Nifty50 and BSE Sensex tanked in trade on Thursday, a day after the stock market saw a record single day gain. While Nifty50 went below 23,800, BSE Sensex was down over 1,000 points intraday.

title: Congress withdraws from Baramati bypoll, 22 independents deny Sunetra Pawar a walkover
content: The Congress has withdrawn its candidate from the Baramati by-election, following an advisory from Sharad Pawar to support NCP candidate Sunetra Pawar. Despite the Congress exit, over 30 independent candidates remain, prompting NCP efforts to persuade them to withdraw before the deadline.

title: ‘Pot of sins now full’: PM Modi attacks TMC’s ‘Nirmam sarkar’ at Asansol rally
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi asserted that a political change in West Bengal is imminent, with citizens demanding it. Addressing a rally in Asansol, he accused the ruling Trinamool Congress of fostering a 'syndicate raj' controlling the coal and sand sectors, vowing a BJP government would restore industrial strength and end mafia rule.

title: Was Lebanon part of ceasefire deal or not? How Pakistan may have muddled US-Iran truce
content: A ceasefire between the US and Iran is shrouded in confusion, with sharp disagreements over its scope, particularly regarding Lebanon. While the US insists the truce is limited to Iran, Pakistan and Iran suggest a broader agreement. This ambiguity, coupled with continued Israeli strikes in Lebanon and differing US negotiation frameworks, threatens the fragile peace ahead of crucial talks.

title: ‘Intent can’t be faulted, but…’: Gavaskar defends Miller after DC’s one-run heartbreak
content: Sunil Gavaskar defended David Miller's intent in Delhi Capitals' narrow IPL loss, emphasising execution challenges in high-pressure moments. He highlighted Ravi Shastri's 1986 tied Test heroics as an example of crucial game awareness. Meanwhile, Rashid Khan's stellar bowling secured Gujarat Titans' first win, though Kevin Pietersen noted overall bowling concerns.

title: Sabarimala case: Centre backs curbs on women’s entry, says 2018 verdict assumed 'men superior'
content: The Centre has told the Supreme Court that the Sabarimala temple's ban on women of menstruating age should be upheld, arguing the 2018 verdict was flawed. Solicitor General Tushar Mehta contended that religious practices aren't solely about gender and cited diverse traditions. The court is examining how equality principles intersect with religious freedom.

title: Ceasefire at risk: Iran closes Hormuz as Israel pounds Lebanon, Hezbollah fires back
content: A fragile Iran-US ceasefire is showing signs of collapse as Iran warns of renewed hostilities amid intensified Israeli strikes in Lebanon. Hezbollah retaliated, accusing Israel of breaching the truce, a stance Israel and the US argue falls outside the agreement's scope, jeopardizing upcoming peace talks.

title: 'Ships continuously coming even amid blockage': Centre assures 100% energy supplies
content: India's LPG supply remains unaffected by West Asian geopolitical tensions, with shipments continuing to arrive through the Strait of Hormuz without disruption. Domestic availability is stable at 100 percent, and the government is closely monitoring developments to ensure energy security and diversification.

title: Securing supply: India presses Iran to expedite movement of oil shipments through Hormuz
content: Despite the temporary pause in hostilities, industry executives have cautioned that a full return to normal oil trade could take at least three months.

title: 'Maine bahut matches finish kiye hai': Kohli comparison agitates Babar
content: Peshawar Zalmi captain Babar Azam sharply dismissed a journalist's comparison to Virat Kohli regarding match-finishing abilities. Despite not finishing the recent PSL victory himself, Azam asserted he has a strong record of closing out games, suggesting the journalist's perception is a misconception.

title: ‘You can’t talk like this,’ observer shoots back at CEC Gyanesh Kumar, gets removed
content: A heated exchange erupted during a high-level election review meeting when a senior observer challenged the Chief Election Commissioner. The observer was subsequently removed, with the EC citing professional incompetence for failing to provide basic details about polling booths. The meeting also addressed vulnerable booths and assured facilities at polling stations.

title: Fuel tanker Green Asha carrying over 15,000 tonnes of LPG reaches India after crossing Hormuz
content: An India-flagged LPG carrier, Green Asha, successfully navigated the Strait of Hormuz and docked at JNPA, carrying vital supplies amid regional tensions. This arrival highlights the ongoing efforts to maintain stable domestic LPG supply, with the government ensuring availability and addressing potential concerns for migrant workers.

title: 'Was to sell it to Tehran': Pakistan PM’s ceasefire statement cleared by WH before release
content: Pakistan's prime minister Shehbaz Sharif's unusual social media post, cleared by the White House, hinted at a coordinated effort to de-escalate US-Iran tensions. The message, calling for a two-week ceasefire, preceded official confirmations of a pause in hostilities. This move, influencing a potential agreement, highlights active backchannel diplomacy amidst public escalation.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' OTT release: When and where to watch Ranveer's film after its theatrical run
content: Ranveer Singh's adventurous sequel, 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge,' has taken the Indian box office by storm, grossing an astonishing ₹1041 crore! For those who want to enjoy the thrill from the comfort of their homes, mark your calendars for its OTT debut on JioHotstar, set to launch soon after IPL 2026 wraps up, around late May or early June 2026. Read on to know more in detail.

title: 'Flying Bam Bahadur’s pigeon': PSL trolled over 'peacemaker act' amid US-Iran ceasefire
content: A symbolic dove release for peace at the PSL 2026 match between Peshawar Zalmi and Hyderabad Kingsmen drew significant online criticism. Critics labelled the 'Pakistan - The Peace Maker' initiative as forced and PR-driven, deeming it out of place during the cricket tournament amidst geopolitical tensions.

title: Will gold rise to Rs 1.65 lakh/10 grams & silver touch Rs 2.70 lakh/kg?
content: Gold and silver price prediction today: Both gold and silver prices are seeing some bullish bias and are likely to see a steady rise in the coming days, says Abhilash Koikkara, Head - Forex & Commodities, Nuvama Professional Clients Group.

title: Will US-Iran ceasefire hold? Trump issues 'bigger, better shooting' warning
content: President Trump issued a stern warning to Iran, stating US forces will remain deployed until a "REAL AGREEMENT" is implemented, threatening a significantly stronger military response if the ceasefire is violated. He reiterated demands for Iran to abandon nuclear weapons and ensure the Strait of Hormuz remains open and safe.

title: Kerala polls 2026: 20 seats that could shape the state’s verdict
content: As Kerala heads to the polls, the election narrative shifts from rallies to booth-level battles. The ruling LDF aims for a historic third term, while the UDF seeks to leverage local body momentum against anti-incumbency. The NDA is focused on converting key pockets into durable political footholds, making these 20 constituencies the state's pressure points.

title: Middle East crisis: Petroleum min Puri to visit Qatar amid energy security concerns
content: Union Minister Hardeep Singh Puri heads to Qatar on April 9-10 amidst Middle East tensions and global energy supply worries. This visit follows a US-Iran ceasefire, but the Strait of Hormuz remains a concern for India's crucial oil imports. The trip aims to secure India's energy interests amid geopolitical uncertainties, as fuel prices have recently risen.

title: IPL: Axar Patel’s gesture towards distraught David Miller wins hearts — watch
content: Delhi Capitals faced a crushing one-run loss to Gujarat Titans after David Miller's valiant effort.  Despite a dramatic chase, a crucial decision on the penultimate ball led to defeat.  However, Delhi Capitals skipper Axar Patel's empathetic gesture towards a dejected David Miller stole the spotlight, showcasing sportsmanship amidst intense competition.

title: Scottie Scheffler brings different vibes to Augusta than other star golfers
content: Scheffler might not generate the same buzz as players like Tiger and Phil, but he's a different kind of superstar, and maybe that's a good thing.

content: Who do our golf experts think will win? Where does our betting expert believe there's value? We break it all down.

title: Bryson bringing 3D-printed 5-iron to Masters
content: Bryson DeChambeau confirmed to ESPN that he'll play the Masters with a 5-iron that he fabricated himself with a 3D printer.

title: Cousins: I shouldn't play if not Raiders' best QB
content: Raiders quarterback Kirk Cousins is aware that Las Vegas is expected to draft Fernando Mendoza and says he only wants to play if it's determined he's the best option.

title: Full of thanks, UCLA celebrates title at Pauley
content: Lauren Betts and her UCLA teammates celebrated the Bruins' first NCAA women's basketball championship with their fans at Pauley Pavilion on Wednesday night.

title: Reports: NCAA to discuss 5-year eligibility model
content: An NCAA panel is scheduled to discuss changes to eligibility rules that would incorporate age into the process, sources said Wednesday.

title: Prosecutors seek Tiger's prescription drug records
content: Prosecutors want the times Tiger Woods' prescriptions were filled, the number of pills, the dosage amounts and any instructions that accompanied the pills, such as warnings about driving while taking them.

title: Prized recruit Hiller, No. 2 OT in '27, picks Florida
content: Maxwell Hiller, the No. 2 offensive tackle and No. 7 overall recruit in the SC Next Junior 300, gave Florida and new head coach Jon Sumrall his verbal pledge Wednesday.

title: Rai 'wins,' young Fleetwood steals show at Par 3
content: Eight-year-old Frankie Fleetwood stole the show at Wednesday's family-friendly Masters Par 3 Contest, which saw Aaron Rai win and four holes-in-one.

content: Between memorizing the astrological signs of his teammates and undergoing extreme underwater training, Brown didn't need to be a hero in Boston. He simply needed to lead. "I'm not going to make myself small ever again."

content: The Deontay Wilder-Derek Chisora fight card caused a major shakeup, but not in the division you would think.

content: Real Madrid and Liverpool are working on deals for Eduardo Camavinga and Alexis Mac Allister to swap clubs. Transfer Talk has the latest.

content: US-Iran War News LIVE Updates:  US President Donald Trump agreed to a two-day ceasefire just hours before the deadline he had set for Iran to capitulate, warning that otherwise a "whole civilisation will die tonight."

content: Joining them in the spirit of the day, another bride from northern Wayanad district also made sure to vote first before heading off to her own wedding ceremony.

content: Asserting that Russia is the main threat to the UK and its allies, UK Defence Secretary John Healey said, "Putin would want us to be distracted by the Middle East."

content: The revenue grew steadily throughout the year. By January 31, the Excise Department had already collected Rs 4,992.69 crore, government data shows.

content: The charts were dated from Feb 28 until Thursday, April 9, and it was unclear if the Guard had cleared any mining on the route since then.

title: Opinion: Opinion | India Sits Atop 500 Million Tons Of Gold Ore. Why Isn't It Being Mined? - By Shashi Tharoor
content: We mine a pittance - barely one and a half tons a year - while draining our foreign exchange reserves to import hundreds of tons annually from mines in Australia, Ghana, and Peru.

content: Having travelled extensively during the campaign, Shashi Tharoor said the momentum on the ground points clearly towards a change in government.

content: The debate around Pakistan's 'mediation' efforts continues. But the fact remains that the country is in the geopolitical limelight for appropriate reasons after a long, long time.

content: The Haryana government has suspended two Indian Administrative Service (IAS) officers in connection with alleged irregularities involving government funds parked with IDFC First Bank

title: US Has Declared Victory, But What Has It Accomplished In Iran?
content: Throughout the over-month-long conflict, Trump has made contradictory statements about his goals for Iran, including putting an end to the country's nuclear program, destroying its military capabilities, and creating regime change.

title: 'Remarks Meant To Influence Assam Poll Process': FIR Against Pawan Khera
content: Congress leader Pawan Khera has alleged that Himanta Sarma's wife has multiple passports and foreign property, which were not declared in the Chief Minister's election affidavit.

title: Traffic Relief For Delhi-Noida-Faridabad As Kalindi Kunj To Get 2 Flyovers
content: The officer said that because of these two flyovers, vehicles will not need to stop at signals, making the junction signal-free.

content: Over 15 cases and chargesheets were filed by the Haryana and Uttar Pradesh police against Yadav - who is accused of extortion, coercive loan settlement of private financiers, among other charges.

content: Transport Minister Mandipalli Ramprasad Reddy inspected buses at Khaja toll plaza in Guntur district. One bus was seized and 15 others were issued challans

content: TCS reported a net profit of ₹12,224 crore in the January-March period year ago, and ₹10,657 crore in the preceding December quarter

content: Committee says there has been an increase in revenue generated by regulatory bodies through application, inspection and licensing fees following the revision of norms

content: Over 2.7 crore voters are eligible to vote across the State, where the elections are being held in a single phase for all 140 constituencies, and 883 candidates are in the fray

content: Mobiles are banned inside polling stations, with provisions made by the Election Commission for depositing the phones, bags and other such items by voters before entering.

content: Police are investigating the possibility that the girl got separated from her group and mistakenly departed the site with other tourists.

content: A senior leader of the Congress, Kidwai had a public career spanning more than six decades; after several terms in the Lok Sabha, she entered the Rajya Sabha in 2004 and remained active in public life well into her eighties

title: T.N. election: AIADMK’s promise of crop loan waiver raises questions of feasibility
content: The AIADMK’s scheme covers crop loans obtained by farmers from primary agricultural credit cooperative societies (PACCS) and loans obtained by persons with disabilities and pavement vendors from cooperative banks.

title: Edappadi K. Palaniswami, the lone star of AIADMK election campaign, steps up his pitch for power
content: The former Chief Minister is the main campaigner for the AIADMK across Tamil Nadu, with a stump speech focused on law and order issues, the State’s finances, concerns of government employees, drug menace, and rising inflation; he also highlights features of the manifesto of his party

title: Regime change in Bengal inevitable, Trinamool’s repository of sins full: Modi
content: PM Narendra Modi spoke about the State’s ‘economic decadence’ and said, “Bengal’s contribution in national GDP has slid to mere five per cent from 12 per cent earlier.”

title: Voter treated as suspect, burdened to prove otherwise: CPI(M) letter to EC on Bengal voter deletions
content: In a letter addressed to Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar, Mr. Baby expressed "anguish, grave concern and strong protest" over the situation

title: Delhi HC rejects Sanjay Bhandari's plea against 'fugitive offender' tag
content: On July 5, 2025, the trial court declared Mr. Bhandari a "fugitive economic offender" on a plea by the Enforcement Directorate, which enables the agency to confiscate all of Mr. Bhandari's assets worth crores of rupees

title: Steps into past: 1,500-year-old reservoir unearthed on Elephanta island
content: Archaeological Survey of India excavation finds sophisticated stepped reservoir and proof of ancient maritime trade

title: Congress slams Centre over withdrawal of bid to host COP33
content: Party says decision on COP33 raises questions over commitment to the Paris Agreement and India’s role in global climate negotiations

title: Poll-bound Assam trails in health and education indicators
content: Low basic vaccination, high maternal mortality, and increasing dropout rates at the higher education level are some of the challenges the State faces

title: Watch: What’s really happening inside the Aam Aadmi Party?
content: What’s going on between Aam Aadmi Party and its leader Raghav Chadha? Amid growing speculation, questions are being raised about his role within the party and the larger political context. We break down the developments, what’s known so far, and what it could mean going forward. To discuss this, we are joined by Sayantan Ghosh, author of The Aam Aadmi Party: The Untold Story of a Political Uprising and Its Undoing.

title: Sri Krishna Sweets trademark dispute: Madras High Court refuses to summon Deputy Registrar
content: Justice Senthilkumar Ramamoorthy says, no useful purpose would be served in summoning the Deputy Registrar of Trademarks to tender evidence regarding a mark registered in 1988

title: Red corner notices issued against Mangaluru gang rape accused and murder accused
content: Sulaiman alias Sameer is an accused in the gang rape of a medical student in Deralakatte in December 2013.

title: PM Modi urges voters to oust ‘nirmam’ TMC government from Bengal; promises six guarantees if BJP wins
content: PM Modi promised that a BJP government in the State will replace the TMC's reign of 'Bhoy' (fear) with that of 'Bharosa' (trust), while all public servants will be made accountable to the people

title: Title ‘Swatantryaveer’ not conferred by government on Savarkar, grandnephew tells court
content: The defamation complaint alleges that Mr. Gandhi made statements against Savarkar on multiple occasions, including a speech on March 5, 2023, at the Overseas Congress event in the United Kingdom.

title: India’s first quantum computing test beds to be set up in Amaravati
content: India’s first quantum computing testing beds, built locally, will be dedicated to the nation on April 14, 2026, officials said

title: Babbar Khalsa International terror module unearthed in Punjab
content: Five hand grenades, two detonators and and improvised explosive device (IED) material was recovered from them

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: A do-or-die battle for major fronts, their skippers
content: Having been in the political wilderness for a decade, the UDF  cannot afford to sit out of power for another five years. For the LDF, which suffered a series of setbacks in the Assembly bypolls, the Lok Sabha elections, and the local body polls, the April 9 Assembly elections will be a test of its credentials.  For the National Democratic Alliance too the polls will be decisive, having seen a slump in their vote share in the local body elections

title: Nagaland: Over 59% voter turnout recorded in Koridang seat till 1 p.m.
content: Six candidates are in the fray for the Assembly bypoll necessitated by the death of sitting BJP MLA Imkong L. Imchen

title: Kerala elections 2026: LDF poised to retain power, says Binoy Viswam
content: Binoy Viswam alleged that key pillars such as democracy, secularism and national sovereignty are being compromised, and claimed that such concerns are shaping voter sentiment

title: PFRDA ropes in private partners to push NPS Swasthya that provides healthcare funding along with retirement planning
content: NPS Swasthya is an initiative intended to provide healthcare funding along with retirement planning. Currently, 10% of the country’s population is in the senior citizen category, and this demographic is expected to climb to over 20% by the year 2045-50

title: BJP, the known unknown in Kerala 2026
content: If the voting gets polarised on the singular question of whether to give Pinarayi Vijayan a third consecutive term, the saffron party could be marginalised

title: Murder case involving speech-and-hearing impaired persons cracked with help from sign language interpreters in Mysuru
content: Mysuru police took the accused to the Institute for Speech and Hearing in Mysuru, and interrogated with the assistance of sign language interpreters; the victim and the accused in the case were speech-and-hearing impaired

title: Battleground Baramati: Congress formally announces party’s withdrawal
content: “As a part of political culture, responding to the emotional appeal, we have decided to respond positively,” Harshwardhan Sapkal said.

title: SC asks states to treat rising child trafficking cases as grave; seeks coordinated action
content: The Bench was irked over the "lackadaisical" approach of several states and UTs in implementing a 2025 judgment aimed at dismantling organised trafficking networks

title: Pre-monsoon rains offer temporary reprieve from forest fires across south Karnataka
content: BRT Tiger reserve in Chamarajanagar district has been witnessing sporadic incidents of fire this year, given scanty rain in the region

title: Puducherry Assembly election: Voter turnout at 37.06% as of 11 a.m.
content: As many as 9.50 lakh electors, spread across the four regions of Puducherry, Karaikal, Mahe and Yanam, are expected to participate as polling advances during the day

title: Assam Assembly elections: Poll official found dead in Sonitpur district
content: Cause of death is yet to be confirmed, though doctors suspect an underlying medical condition, says an official

title: Morning vs. evening: how do the benefits of yoga differ depending on when you do it
content: Participants who practised yoga in the morning reported a greater reduction in sleep disturbances and a shift towards ‘morningness’ — a preference for early-day activity aligned with natural biological rhythms, according to a new study

title: Ex-Union Minister Abu Hasem Khan Choudhury dies at 89
content: Abu Hasem Khan Choudhury had been suffering from multiple age-related complications. He died at a private hospital in Kolkata.

title: Kerala Assembly elections: Shashi Tharoor says high voter turnout augurs well for Congress-led UDF
content: Tharoor said high participation suggests people are ‘voting for change’, which he said could only come through the ‘replacement’ of LDF government with a UDF administration after 10 years in power

title: Karnataka Minister Eshwar Khandre proposes sterilisation of select wildlife species
content: The Minister said that this measure is only a proposal, and that there should be a larger public debate on the issue

title: HYDRAA demolishes two-storey structure encroaching road in Hyderabad’s Bansilalpet
content: The encroachment began as a small shop on the ground floor with a residence above but expanded soon after

title: Rankings: Profiling more than 60 wide receivers
content: Mike Clay is revealing his fantasy football rookie rankings at each key offensive position . the rankings do not include rookies because we are unsure of their landing spots . all references to player ages are as of the season opener on sept. 9, 2026 .

title: Women's transfer rankings for 2026-27 season
content: the women's college basketball transfer portal opened Monday with a tidal wave of activity . more than 1,300 players are looking for new homes . the portal closes April 20 but players' decisions for their next school can still come after that .

title: PSG's wastefulness leaves door open, but this isn't Klopp's Liverpool any more
content: Ousmane Dembélé has a history of giving Liverpool hope when all seems lost in a UEFA Champions League knockout tie . in 2019, with Barcelona 3-0 up in the dying seconds of their semifinal first leg against Liverpool at Camp Nou, dembélé blazed an easy chance to make it 4-0 over the crossbar . we all know what happened next .

title: Coaches, GMs weigh in on SEC's top newcomers
content: new faces will be announced all over the SEC in 2026 . coaches, general managers and personnel staffers selected one newcomer for each SEC team with a path to immediate contribution . RB Ezavier and RB ezavier are among the players to add to the roster .

title: 'I'm going to put it in my house in a safe': How the Masters lawn gnome became so popular that it might be discontinued
content: Ryan Carey is considered the world's most accomplished golf memorabilia expert . the gnome has been a kitschy decorative item for centuries . it's the only item available on site that is limited to one purchase per patron .

title: WNBA mock draft: UConn's Fudd drops to No. 3 pick
content: Olivia Miles drops 28 points, 10 rebounds and eight assists as TCU puts away Virginia 79-69 . the college players available in Monday's draft had one last audition in the tournament . in our latest WNBA mock draft, for example, Miles projected as a lottery pick last season when she was eligible age-wise .

title: Pochettino's to-do list: What the USMNT must figure out before World Cup
content: Mauricio Pochettino will have a pre-World Cup camp to get his side ready for competition . he'll have to lock in his choices on which players will join him in the world cup cauldron . the men's national team are ranked outside the top 20 contenders to win the 2026 world cup .

title: Way-Too-Early Top 25: Ranking the best teams for next season, now
content: the 2026-27 college basketball season is expected to take place in 2026 . we're already rolling right into the "offseason," which mostly involves rampant roster changes for the next six weeks . in the past, we've had pretty hard-and-fast house rules regarding the Way-Too-Early Top 25 .

title: NBA teams all want to play faster -- but it might be making them worse
content: a record 18 teams are averaging at least 100 possessions per game . the new york Knicks wanted to play faster this season . they wanted to trade the dance team, concession workers and anyone besides Jalen Brunson to the Bucks .

title: NC State men recount trainer's alleged sexual abuse
content: former athletes say they suffered silently through misconduct under the guise of medical treatment . alleged misconduct included touching their genitals inappropriately and subjecting them to non-standard drug testing procedures . one former athlete says he went to Murphy with back pain and was subjected to an unwelcome treatment that involved the trainer's hands in his shorts .

title: 'Don't move on, move forward': How hockey helped Michael Hage overcome tragedy
content: the path to a national title runs through the site of the Frozen Four . it was in Las Vegas where one of the most significant moments of Hage's hockey career occurred . the 19-year-old center doesn't remember everything about his draft night at the Sphere in June 2024 .

title: Re-picking the 2016 NHL draft: Who goes in the top 3?
content: it has been almost 10 years since the 2016 NHL draft was held in Buffalo . the team reshuffled the picks with hindsight, taking into account what teams were looking for at the time and how these players have performed for the past decade . Rachel Kryshak: I was watching my childhood team tear things up in hopes of getting an elite goal scorer .

title: 2026 NBA draft top 100: Where top prospects land on the big board after March
content: yaxel Lendeborg wins a national championship with Michigan . AJ Dybantsa takes that mantle as the dust has settled on the season . the top of the draft remains open for discussion, with a group of talented players still jockeying for position in the lottery .
